decks.notes: This deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures while disrupting opponent's strategies with hate cards like Archon of Emeria and Chancellor of the Annex. By utilizing powerful mana acceleration like Chrome Mox and fast mana sources such as Black Lotus and Mana Crypt, the deck aims to quickly deploy its threats and lock down the opponent's plays with cards like Void Mirror. The inclusion of cards like Cavern of Souls and Karakas provide additional protection and utility, making it difficult for the opponent to effectively deal with the deck's game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing Paradoxical Outcome to generate mana and draw cards, allowing for explosive plays and setting up powerful combos. It features a variety of fast mana sources like Moxen and Mana Crypt, along with card draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Merchant Scroll. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to control the game until it can assemble its combo pieces. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a companion to provide additional value and flexibility. Overall, the deck aims to quickly and efficiently assemble its combo pieces to overwhelm the opponent.

This deck focuses on utilizing the Companion ability of Lurrus of the Dream-Den to maximize the efficiency of low-cost creatures and disruption spells. It aims to establish early board presence with aggressive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Mai, Scornful Striker while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares. The deck also contains a number of mana artifacts and land disruption cards like Wasteland to control the flow of the game. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent while maintaining control of the board.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Bazaar of Baghdad to quickly fill up the graveyard with Dredge cards like Golgari Thug and Stinkweed Imp, allowing for explosive plays with cards like Hollow One and Ichorid. Serum Powder helps dig for combo pieces while Force of Will and Force of Negation provide protection. Creeping Chill provides additional reach, while cards like Narcomoeba and Prized Amalgam help to create a strong board presence. The deck aims to use these synergies to overwhelm opponents and win the game quickly through the graveyard interactions.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting opponent's strategies while generating card advantage and resource denial. With powerful counter spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize, along with removal spells like Fatal Push, the deck aims to control the game tempo and protect its key threats like Snapcaster Mage and Psychic Frog. The inclusion of powerful card draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time, along with disruptive cards like Null Rod and Strip Mine, further solidifies the deck's ability to outmaneuver opponents and grind out a victory in the long game.

The deck focuses on using powerful control elements like counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain dominance over the game. It also utilizes card advantage through draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. The inclusion of a companion, Lurrus of the Dream-Den, provides additional flexibility and value throughout the game. The mana base is optimized for consistent access to necessary colors and includes powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Moxen to ramp into game-changing plays. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent for victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game with a mix of counterspells, removal, and disruptive cards while utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes cards like Wrenn and Six for value, Boseiju, Who Endures for uncounterable spells, and various cards for card advantage like Treasure Cruise and Dig Through Time. By disrupting the opponent's game plan and deploying powerful threats ahead of schedule, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly with powerful artifacts like Moxen and Tolarian Academy, while utilizing Paradoxical Outcome to draw cards and replay key artifacts. It includes a mix of disruptive spells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo, along with powerful cards for card advantage like Ancestral Recall and Snapcaster Mage. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional recursion of key artifacts from the graveyard. The goal is to assemble a winning combination of artifacts and spells to overwhelm the opponent quickly.

This deck revolves around quickly filling the graveyard with cards like Golgari Grave-Troll, Golgari Thug, and Stinkweed Imp to enable graveyard recursion and combo off with cards like Narcomoeba, Ichorid, and Prized Amalgam. Using cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder to dig for combo pieces, along with disruptive elements like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect the combo. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with fast graveyard recursion and explosive plays, while disrupting opponent's strategies with cards like Chalice of the Void and Grief.

The main strategy of the deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and disruptive creatures like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Psychic Frog. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to maintain a strong board presence while controlling the pace of the game. The inclusion of cards like Ancestral Recall, Force of Will, and the Mox artifacts allow for powerful card advantage and tempo plays, while cards like Swords to Plowshares provide efficient creature removal. The deck's goal is to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on using the powerful enchantment Oath of Druids to cheat out big creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier early in the game. It also includes control elements such as Force of Will, Thoughtseize, and Oko, Thief of Crowns to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, the deck utilizes cards like Show and Tell and Flash to quickly put threats into play. Overall, the deck aims to control the game while leveraging the powerful effects of Oath of Druids to gain an advantage and dominate the battlefield.

The deck focuses on controlling the game while utilizing the powerful Oath of Druids card to put a large creature like Griselbrand into play quickly. With a mix of fast mana sources, card draw, and counterspells, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own win condition. By using cards like Show and Tell and Tinker, the deck can quickly assemble powerful combinations to outpace the opponent. Additionally, the deck includes versatile cards like Mystical Tutor and Demonic Tutor to search for key pieces when needed.

This deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive artifact creatures and disruptive artifacts, using cards like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim, Nettlecyst, and Thorn of Amethyst to control the board and apply pressure. The deck also utilizes efficient mana acceleration through cards like Black Lotus, Moxen, and Ancient Tomb to quickly deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Crucible of Worlds and Null Rod. The goal is to establish an early advantage and maintain control of the game by leveraging powerful artifacts and mana denial strategies.

The deck revolves around utilizing powerful artifacts and mana acceleration to assemble a winning combination that focuses on drawing cards, generating mana, and ultimately gaining control of the game. Key cards such as The One Ring, Paradoxical Outcome, and Coveted Jewel are used to create a loop of card draw and mana generation, allowing the player to outvalue and outpace their opponent. The inclusion of disruptive elements like Trinisphere and counterspells such as Force of Will adds additional layers of protection and disruption to the deck's game plan.

This deck focuses on using low-cost creatures and disruptive spells to control the game tempo while constantly applying pressure on the opponent. Using cards like Guide of Souls and Mockingbird to quickly build a board presence, combined with disruptive cards like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Force of Negation to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares helps to clear the way for your creatures to attack, ultimately aiming to win the game through quick and aggressive plays while controlling the opponent's resources.

The deck focuses on utilizing control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while efficiently drawing cards and generating value. It includes a mix of powerful spells and key permanents like Orcish Bowmasters and Lavinia, Azorius Renegade to control the board and slow down the opponent. The deck also has access to powerful cards like Ancestral Recall, Black Lotus, and Demonic Tutor to further enhance its control capabilities. By carefully managing resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to outvalue and outplay the opponent in a Vintage 1 vs 1 format.
